_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of lawful bases for data processing under applicable regulations, including consent requirements, contractual necessity, legal obligations, vital interests, public task authority, and legitimate interest balancing tests.
comments: NICE knowledge intersects with WIDAI RC domain. Knowledge RC-K-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-K-010 (SP, cosine: 0.423), RC-K-006 (RC, cosine: 0.422).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.8/10 (raw 0.281). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge]. NICE-only terms [decryption] vs WIDAI-only terms [applicable, authority, balancing, bases, consent, contractual] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.848 (P=0.892, R=0.808). P exceeds R by 0.083 — the NICE element's 3 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 27 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.0%). "Knowledge of decryption" and "Knowledge of lawful bases for data processing under applicable regulations, incl" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.455 > STS 0.281 (gap +0.174). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [knowledge] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.1%. Shared tokens: [knowledge]. NICE-only: [decryption]. WIDAI-only: [applicable, authority, balancing, bases, consent, contractual].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.174 with low STS 0.281. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
